body_txt: This character is the local winner of the Superpower Lottery — either the World's Strongest Man or damn close to it. They have enough power to be able to succeed or accomplish almost anything... ...if they weren't a complete and utter moron . Capable of using Flight to go anywhere they want? Doesn't matter, because they get lost easily . Can shrug off most attacks or weapons with their Super-Toughness ? Irrelevant because any tiny injury will make them panic and cry. Able to chase anyone down with their Super-Speed ? Too bad that they get distracted too easily. And this all assumes they even know what powers they have; Forgot About His Powers is the norm for this guy. The best way to think of this trope is not as a character trope, but a narrative trope about failure . While it can overlap with Unskilled, but Strong or Dumb Muscle , it's not quite the same. Those archetypes can still win if they hit hard enough that their relative lack of skill or planning doesn't matter. At the very least, their allies or teammates can help use traps or strategies that enable them to unleash their massive power at the best time. The character described by this trope, however, is so incompetent that people weaker than them are either more reliable teammates or regularly defeat these idiots with little trouble. Planning or help from others often won't work either, because this idiot will find some way to fail even the most basic role . They may also be Lethally Stupid , and thus almost as much of a danger to their allies as the enemy is. In anyone else's hands, their abilities would be a Story-Breaker Power ... but for better or worse, in this character's hands, they might as well be stage tricks. Merely being Incompletely Trained , having a Weaksauce Weakness or losing ONE fight to someone better is not this trope. This character fails despite their amount of training, or their flaws make them impossible to train in the first place. Other characters with the same weaknesses have either overcome them or at the very least learned to work around them. Their losses and failures are total embarrassments that someone of their power or level should have been able to handle. Failure has to be the norm for this character, or so common that no matter how skilled or powerful they become, the outcome is never certain. NOTE: To qualify as this trope, the example MUST explain how stupidity either negatively-affects the use of the character's powers or negatively-affects the outcomes of their usual battles/missions. Compare Idiot Hero and Brains Versus Brawn , with the trope referring to the "Brawn" side, as well as Almighty Idiot for cases where the power is godlike but the foolishness below human levels. Also compare and contrast the Almighty Janitor . Compare Super Weapon, Average Joe , for when the user is weak and incompetent, but their weapon is mighty. May overlap with Inept Mage , whose spells and abilities tend to backfire due to incompetence. Also see Power Incontinence , when someone doesn't know how to turn their powers on or off. Contrast Heart Is an Awesome Power . A potential consequence of Giving the Sword to a Noob or Skipping the Basics . One way it's demonstrated that it's mere incompetence holding a character back is when an Alternate Self is just that much better . Do not confuse with Powerful, but Inaccurate . The exact polar inverse is Invincible Incompetent . Contrast with Powerful and Helpless , who may not be incompetent for all that it (doesn't) saves them.
